properties.note:
ACS Infrastructure Fort Worth Data Center Project
ACS Infrastructure Development Inc., a U.S. subsidiary of the Madrid-based global infrastructure giant ACS Group, is planning a major greenfield data center campus in northwest Fort Worth, Texas. ACS Group, which operates in over 25 countries and employs more than 135,000 people, is expanding into digital infrastructure as part of its strategy to develop 1.1 GW of initial capacity across multiple regions, including 300 MW in the U.S. The Fort Worth project aligns with this push, though specific power capacity details for the site have not been disclosed.
Project Overview
AspectDetailsDeveloperACS Infrastructure Development Inc. (ACS Group subsidiary; U.S. operations often via Turner Construction)Site Location107-acre greenfield site near Hicks Field Road, west of I-35, north of I-820, and east of Business 287 (Council District 7). Key addresses: 10059 Hicks Field Road, 10197 Hicks Field Road, 9401 Wagley Robertson Road. Near North Saginaw Road intersection.ScopeFive-building data center campus + privately owned Oncor Electric substation. Purpose-built, single-story data halls with a two-story administrative building.Phases- Phase 1: Completion by Dec. 31, 2031 ($240.8M real property + $842.8M business personal property; 28 jobs).
- Phase 2: Completion by Dec. 31, 2034 ($240.8M real property + $842.8M business personal property; 9 additional jobs).Total InvestmentMinimum $2 billion ($481M in real property improvements + $1.7B in business personal property).Jobs Created37 full-time positions at full build-out (average annual salary ≥ $150,000).Sustainability FeaturesWater loop system to minimize water usage; focus on reducing noise impact near residential areas via physical buffers.Tenants/CustomersNone secured yet; ACS plans to develop for lease or sale. Past clients include Meta, Microsoft, Google, Apple, and AWS.StatusPlanned; filings submitted in April 2025. Fort Worth City Council approved economic incentives in April 2025 (35% tax abatement for Phase 1, up to 70% for Phase 2 on incremental business personal property value). Construction not yet started.
This project contributes to the Dallas-Fort Worth area's data center boom, with 141 facilities already in the region, driven by low energy costs and AI/cloud demand. ACS has local ties through Turner Construction, which built projects like DFW Airport's Terminal F and TCU's Amon G. Carter Stadium expansion. For more details, refer to ACS Group's investor presentations or Fort Worth Economic Development resources.955ms

properties.name: Atos Group: Texas Data Center
properties.note: Atos Group Texas Data Center is located at 5920 Windhaven Pkwy suite 120, Plano, TX 75093, USA. The gross building size for this location has not been provided. The gross colocation space is not available for this data center. No power information has been offered for this location. We found 154 data center locations within 50 miles of this facility. Certifications for this location include HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 1, SOC 2.

properties.name: AUS2- AUS3
properties.note:
Located in the heart of Austin in the Met Center business park just seven minutes from Austin-Bergstrom International Airport, CyrusOne's AUS2 and AUS3 data centers provide a combined 242,000 sq. ft. of IT technical space. The data centers' infrastructure combines multiple levels of technology and security, resulting in no single point of failure.

The location is ideal for primary production systems backup and the synchronous data replication requirements of mission-critical applications. CyrusOne offers powered shells to provide customers with a fast time-to-market option, including fully redundant support systems, scalable power levels, and connectivity options.

properties.name: DFW1
properties.note:
CyrusOne's DFW1 data center, located in the Dallas suburb of Carrollton, TX, is the largest facility of its kind in Texas, boasting 600,000 sq. ft. of technical space and state-of-the-art energy efficiency technologies. The data center is complemented by Class-A office space, including large-scale suites.

The DFW1 data center features zero-water consumption cooling and uses highly efficient multiple 160-ton Direct Evaporative Cooling Units with rapid restart and adaptive controlled microprocessors. The facility serves as the data center of choice for the 911 operations of several local cities that form the North Texas Emergency Communications Center (NTECC).

properties.name: DFW2
properties.note:
CyrusOne’s 200,000-square-foot, DFW2 data center provides redundant power architecture, high-density capability, and an abundance of connectivity to many local and international companies.

CyrusOne’s DFW2 data center is conveniently located just 9 minutes from Dallas Fort Worth (DFW) International Airport in Lewisville, Texas, and within the Convergence Technology Center.

Dallas is an excellent market for colocation due to its central location, low risk for natural disasters, available fiber, and low cost of operatin

properties.name: DFW3-DFW5
properties.note:
Located in the Dallas suburb of Allen, TX, CyrusOne's data center campus comprised of DFW3, DFW4 and DFW5 offers a massive 976,000 sq. ft. of technical space at full build-out. The facility is purpose-built, offering industry-leading efficiencies, the latest cooling technology, uniquely low PUEs, and multilayer security.

With over 90 acres, CyrusOne's Allen, Texas facilities make up the largest data center campus in Texas. Sustainability features include water-free cooling and the sourcing of renewable power, while robust security includes on-site security guards and anti-tailgating vehicle entry.

properties.name: SAT1
properties.note:
Located west of downtown San Antonio off state highway 151, CyrusOne's 110,000-sq. ft. SAT1 data center was the city's first enterprise colocation facility. The SAT1 data center boasts some of the lowest power costs in the country and can scale up to 20 megawatts of power from multiple substations.

The facility provides extra layers of physical security including hurricane shutters, impact-resistant glazing, and extra diesel on-site, and is located outside of flood zones. Features include 24x7x365 security onsite, video surveillance of the exterior and interior, and rigid access control to ensure the safety and security of mission-critical environments.

properties.name: ACT1 - Waco Data Center
properties.note:
Discover the thriving business environment of the Waco metro area, boasting diversity, natural resources, and top-notch infrastructure. This dynamic region continually adapts to support local businesses, fostering growth and attracting talent and investments.

With DataBank's strategically positioned Waco data center, located just a short distance from Houston, you gain the advantage of resilience against natural disasters and the perfect setup for business continuity and disaster recovery. Additionally, this facility acts as a gateway to DataBank's Dallas-area data centers, allowing seamless access for mission-critical operations.

Situated in the heart of downtown Waco, the ACT1 data center offers easy access to a variety of amenities, including restaurants and hotels. As a reliable disaster recovery backup, it serves major Texas cities such as Austin, Dallas, Houston, and San Antonio. Don't miss the opportunity to secure office space on the 3rd floor.

properties.name: DFW 26
properties.note: Digital Convergence comprises 829,372 sq. ft. on 168.4 acres, including 60 acres of additional land for future development, located in Lewisville, Texas, which competes in the Northwest Dallas submarket. The property was originally developed by Texas Instruments. The campus consists of ten large-box building connected to a central utility plant (CUP) by a central spine. Buildings 1, 11 and part of 3 are two stories with the remaining buildings single-story

properties.name: GIPNetworks Plano/Synergy Park
properties.note: GI Partners purchased from Flexential in 2016

properties.name: Google Midlothian Data Center
properties.note:
In June of 2019, Google officially broke ground on its $600 million data center in Midlothian, Texas. Once fully operational, our data center will employ a number of people in a variety of full-time and contractor roles, including computer technicians, engineers, and various food services, maintenance, and security roles.

Since 2011, we've awarded more than $4 million to Texas schools and nonprofits.

properties.name: Lincoln Rackhouse: Plano Data Center
properties.note:
The Plano Data Center by Lincoln Rackhouse is a state-of-the-art facility located in Plano, Texas, a thriving business and technology hub within the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex. Designed to deliver exceptional reliability, connectivity, and scalability, the Plano Data Center provides businesses with robust digital infrastructure to support mission-critical operations and evolving IT demands.

Key Features:
Resilient Infrastructure: Engineered for maximum uptime, the data center features redundant power systems, advanced cooling technologies, and high-performance IT equipment to ensure seamless operations.
Carrier-Neutral Connectivity: Offers access to a diverse ecosystem of network providers, enabling low-latency, high-speed connections to regional, national, and global markets.
Comprehensive Security: Multi-layered security protocols, including biometric access controls, 24/7 surveillance, and real-time threat detection, safeguard physical and digital assets.
Energy Efficiency: The facility integrates energy-efficient cooling systems and optimized power usage effectiveness (PUE) to align with sustainability goals while maintaining operational excellence.
Flexible Solutions: Customizable colocation options, private suites, and modular configurations allow businesses to scale their IT infrastructure according to their unique requirements.
Strategic Location:
Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex: Positioned in Plano, a key hub for industries such as technology, finance, and healthcare, offering access to a rich talent pool and a vibrant business ecosystem.
Central Connectivity: Strategically located to provide efficient data transfer and connectivity across the U.S., with direct links to major international markets.
Benefits:
Ideal for enterprises requiring secure, scalable, and high-performance data center solutions.
Supports industries with demanding compliance and security needs, including healthcare, finance, and cloud computing.
Backed by Lincoln Rackhouse’s proven expertise in delivering innovative and reliable data center management.
The Plano Data Center by Lincoln Rackhouse is a trusted partner for organizations seeking advanced, scalable, and secure IT solutions. With its strategic location, robust features, and sustainable design, it empowers businesses to achieve their operational and digital transformation goals.

properties.name: Waco 1 Data Center
properties.note:
The Waco 1 Data Center by Lumen Technologies is a secure, enterprise-class facility located in Waco, Texas, strategically positioned between the major metropolitan hubs of Dallas-Fort Worth, Austin, and Houston. Serving as a vital regional infrastructure node, this facility delivers resilient colocation, scalable network connectivity, and high-availability IT infrastructure for businesses across central Texas and beyond.

Engineered to meet Tier III standards, the Waco 1 Data Center is equipped with redundant power systems, including dual utility feeds, uninterruptible power supplies (UPS), and backup generators to ensure continuous uptime. The site is supported by N+1 precision cooling systems, which maintain stable thermal conditions for high-density and performance-critical workloads.

Security is a top priority at Waco 1, with 24/7 on-site security personnel, biometric access controls, multi-layer video surveillance, and advanced fire detection and suppression systems safeguarding the facility. It supports compliance with key industry standards, including SOC 1 & 2, PCI DSS, and HIPAA, making it ideal for organizations in regulated industries such as healthcare, financial services, and government.

As a carrier-neutral facility, Waco 1 offers access to a diverse ecosystem of telecom providers, ISPs, and cloud service platforms. Direct integration with the Lumen global IP backbone provides low-latency, high-speed connectivity to major markets throughout Texas and across North America, supporting hybrid cloud deployments, disaster recovery architectures, and edge computing initiatives.

With its central geographic location, enterprise-grade infrastructure, and seamless network access, the Waco 1 Data Center is a reliable and scalable solution for businesses seeking robust digital infrastructure in a stable, strategically situated environment.

properties.name: Meta, Inc.: Fort Worth Data Center
properties.note: Facebook’s new Fort Worth Data Center is a new, 200-acre data campus being delivered in multiple phases. The site includes three IT load data centers with rooftop air handling equipment, outdoor standby diesel generators and supporting electrical substation. An administrative building connects two of the data centers; the third data center is a standalone building.

properties.note: $1.1B hyperscaler-grade data center. Fort Worth Zoning Commission approved zoning changes Apr 8, 2026 (KERA News); City Council abatement vote tabled Apr 2 per Fort Worth Star-Telegram. Council final vote expected. Added by weekly intel agent.
